title: Festuca romoi
taxonomy: Festuca romoi is a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It is a type of tussock-forming grass, commonly found in mountainous regions. The classification of Festuca romoi is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Monocots, order - Poales, family - Poaceae, genus - Festuca, species - F. romoi. Festuca romoi is closely related to other species in the Festuca genus, such as Festuca altaica and Festuca gracilior. Its classification is also part of the broader classification of grasses, which includes over 10,000 different species.

intro: Festuca romoi, also known as Römö fescue, is a species of grass native to the island of Römö in Germany. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and supporting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Festuca romoi include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often consumed to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of insomnia.
